title: GatewayTransferNative local-delivery swaps spend the pre-fee amount and can siphon reserves
reason: Merged into existing F-001; this is the already-accepted pre-fee branch of the broader attacker-controlled swap-parameter reserve-drain issue.

title: GatewaySend destination settlement trusts nominal ERC20 pull amounts and can spend reserves on taxed or soft-failing tokens
reason: Not kept separately because authenticated `GatewaySend.onCall` execution is presently bricked by F-026, so this destination-side settlement path is unreachable in the current integration.

title: Cross-chain withdrawal logic never binds `dstChainId` to `targetZRC20` and can route funds through the wrong chain-specific path
reason: Rejected as a user-controlled misconfiguration issue; the actual destination route is determined by `targetZRC20`, and the code support here is insufficient for a cross-user exploit, reserve drain, or permissionless protocol-level DoS.

title: Native-input `withdrawToNativeChain` lets callers bypass platform fees by decoupling `amount` from `msg.value`
reason: Merged into F-004; it is another consequence of the same nominal-amount-versus-actual-funding bug in `withdrawToNativeChain`.

title: GatewaySend depositAndCall does not verify swap output or return funds on swap failure
reason: Rejected; swap failure reverts the whole transaction, and low output is governed by user-supplied DODO parameters rather than a distinct protocol bug.

title: GatewaySend onCall uses message amount instead of actual token balance
reason: Not kept separately because authenticated `GatewaySend.onCall` execution is presently bricked by F-026, so this destination-side path is unreachable in the current integration.

title: GatewayTransferNative.claimRefund reads storage after delete allowing double-claim with reentrancy
reason: Rejected as an inaccurate conflation of two existing issues: reentrancy before delete is already F-006, while post-delete storage reads only affect event data and are already F-025.

title: Platform fee calculation ignores token decimals causing severe undercharging
reason: Rejected; fee calculation on raw token units is standard and does not inherently mis-handle decimals.

title: Public withdraw function in GatewayTransferNative lacks access control and can be abused
reason: Merged into existing F-008; this is the same public-withdraw-plus-residual-allowance issue already captured.

title: No deadline check in Uniswap swap allowing stale trades
reason: Rejected; the code passes `block.timestamp + MAX_DEADLINE` at execution time, so this is not a separate stale-deadline bug.

title: onRevert uses .transfer for ETH which can DoS smart contract receivers
reason: Rejected as incorrect; the relevant bug is the missing native-refund branch already captured by F-017, not use of Solidity `.transfer` in `onRevert`.

title: No event emitted for critical withdrawToNativeChain state change
reason: Rejected as non-reportable operational telemetry rather than protocol-level security harm.

title: Message decoding lacks bounds checking allowing out-of-bounds read
reason: Rejected; malformed short payloads revert during slicing or produce unusable zero values, with no supported theft, lockup, or cross-user exploit path.

title: External call to DODORouteProxy lacks reentrancy protection
reason: Rejected; `DODORouteProxy` is an owner-configured trusted integration here, and no concrete reentrant state-corruption path beyond already-reported issues is supported by the code.
